evidence.title: Indian Express report on Iran western airspace closure, May 23 2026
evidence.source_description: Indian Express article page text captured by web search/open output, reporting Iran Civil Aviation Authority NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 and commercial flight effects.
evidence.relevance: Credible secondary reporting corroborating the NOTAM source, Iran Civil Aviation Authority issuance, May 22-May 25 window, eight-airport exception list including listed market airports, daylight-only restrictions, fresh CAA approval requirement, and commercial rerouting.

evidence.title: FAA International NOTAM Q-code decode for QFALC
evidence.source_description: FAA Order 7930.2 appendix B page text, International NOTAM (Q) Codes, opened 2026-06-19. Relevant lines: Q-code groups use second/third letters for subject and fourth/fifth for status; FA means Aerodrome; LC means Closed.
evidence.relevance: Decodes plaintiff's PX-2 NOTAM code QFALC as an aerodrome-closure notice, relevant to whether the notice proves an airspace-region closure or only airport closures/permissions.

evidence.title: Notamify capture of Iran NOTAM OIIX A1010/26
evidence.source_description: Notamify page for OIIX NOTAM A1010/26, including raw NOTAM text and interpretation as captured in web search/open output.
evidence.relevance: Primary-like NOTAM archive capture showing Iran OIIX A1010/26 effective 2026-05-22 20:46 UTC to 2026-05-25 08:30 UTC, closing airports in the west part of Tehran FIR with limited listed-airport exceptions and CAA permission requirement for civil IFR passenger flights.

evidence.title: OurAirports Iran airport code extract for market-listed and NOTAM-excepted airports
evidence.source_description: OurAirports Iran airports CSV retrieved with curl and filtered for the five market-listed airports plus the eight airport identifiers excepted in PX-2 NOTAM A1010/26.
evidence.relevance: Shows the ICAO/IATA mapping for the market-listed airports and the airports excepted in PX-2. This is relevant because PX-2's exception list includes only OISS/SYZ among the five market-listed airports, while plaintiff's secondary report says several listed airports remained operational.
